‚ÄúA Chinese woman [surname Yan] was 
offered two refunds from Apple for her 
new iPhone X‚Ä¶ [it] was unable to tell her 
and her other Chinese colleague apart.‚ÄĚ
‚ÄúThinking that a faulty camera was to 
blame, the store operator gave [Yan] a 
refund, which she used to purchase 
another iPhone X. But the new phone 
turned out to have the same problem, 
prompting the store worker to offer her 
another refund ‚Ä¶ It is unclear whether she 
purchased a third phone‚ÄĚ
Source: https://www.newsweek.com/iphone-x-racist-apple-refunds-device-cant-tell-chinese-people-apart-woman-751263

https://developers.google.com/machine-learning/crash-course/classification/roc-and-auc
Demo

Different Types 
of Performance 
Metrics
ÔāĖThus far, for binary classification tasks, we have largely only 
been concerned with error rate i.e., minimizing the 0-1 loss
ÔāĖError rate can be problematic in settings with‚Ä¶
ÔāĖImbalanced labels
ÔāĖAsymmetric costs for different types of errors
ÔāĖSome common alternatives are
ÔāĖFalse positive rate (FPR) = FP / N = FP / (FP + TN)
ÔāĖFalse negative rate (FNR) = FN / P = FN / (TP + FN)
ÔāĖPositive predictive value (PPV) = TP / PP = TP / (TP + FP)
ÔāĖNegative predictive value (NPV) = TN / PN = TN / (FN + TN)

This is one possible definition of unfairness. 
We‚Äôll explore a few others and see how they relate to one another.

Source: https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4533047 
‚ÄúHowever, when it came to race, judges appeared to misapply the AI guidance. Ho found judges 
generally sentenced Black and White defendants equally harshly based on their risk scores alone. 
But when the AI recommended probation for low-risk offenders, judges disproportionately 
declined to offer alternatives to incarceration for Black defendants.
As a result, similar Black offenders ended up with significantly fewer alternative punishments and 
longer average jail terms than their White counterparts ‚ÄĒ missing out on probation by 6% and 
receiving jail terms averaging a month longer.‚ÄĚ
Source: https://news.tulane.edu/pr/ai-sentencing-cut-jail-time-low-risk-offenders-study-finds-racial-bias-persisted

Key Takeaways
ÔāĖHigh-profile cases of algorithmic bias are increasingly 
common as machine learning is applied more broadly in a 
variety of contexts
ÔāĖVarious definitions of fairness
ÔāĖSelection rate parity (Independence): ‚ĄéūĚĎč, ūĚźī‚ä•ūĚźī

ÔāĖIn all but the simplest of cases, any two of these 
three are mutually exclusive
